DISCOVERY, DEPT. 301, AS TO THE FEB-03-2025 HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS TRILOBIO INC., ROYA AMINI-NAIENI, AND MAXIMILIAN SCHOMMER'S MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND FOR SANCTIONS; THE COUT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. THE COURT GRANTS THE MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RFP RESPONSES, DENIES THE MOTION TO DEEM OBJECTIONS WAIVED EXCEPT AS TO SPECIFIED RFPS, AND GRANTS SANCTIONS (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING). COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING WITH A COVER LETTER OUTLINING COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (301/CVA)

DISCOVERY, DEPT. 301, AS TO THE FEB-03-2025 HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS TRILOBIO INC., ROYA AMINI-NAIENI, AND MAXIMILIAN SCHOMMER'S MOTION TO APPOINT DISCOVERY REFEREE; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. TRILOBIO REQUESTS A DISCOVERY REFEREE. GANDALL OPPOSES ON THE GROUNDS THAT HE HAS RETAINED NEW COUNSEL AND WILL COMPLY WITH HIS DISCOVERY OBLIGATIONS GOING FORWARD. THE COURT DENIES THE MOTION WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO ANY RENEWED MOTION FOR A DISCOVERY REFEREE OR FURTHER APPROPRIATE RELIEF AFTER TRILOBIO HAS REVIEWED THE RESPONSES GANDALL HAS BEEN ORDERED TO PROVIDE. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING WITH A COVER LETTER OUTLINING COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (301/CVA)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jul-01-2025 Vacated

Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery Requests
Off Calendar May-01-2025 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANTS TRILOBIO INC., ROYA AMINI-NAIENI,AND MAXIMILIAN SCHOMMER's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery Requests.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Off calendar. Defendants Trilobio Inc. et al. filed a notice of motion but did not file a memorandum of points and authorities, separate statement, or declarations in support.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Oct-23-2024 Continued To Jul-14-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem The Truth Of All Matters In Trilobio Requests For Admission Be Admitted And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-03-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANTS TRILOBIO INC., ROYA AMINI-NAIENI, AND MAXIMILIAN SCHOMMER's Motion To Deem The Truth Of All Matters In Trilobio Requests For Admission Be Admitted And For Sanctions.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The court grants the motion to compel further RFA responses, denies the motion to deem RFAs admitted, and grants sanctions.

The requests at issue are reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (CCP Section 2017.010.) Trilobio has shown Gandall's responses are incomplete. Gandall has not demonstrated that any objection has merit. The court orders Gandall to provide further responses to RFAs set 1, Nos. 1-10 within 10 court days from notice of entry of this order.

The court denies the motion to deem the RFAs admitted. Trilobio characterizes Gandall's responses untimely because the responses provided by the extended deadline were not substantive, a purported condition of the extension agreement. The Discovery Act provides a "deemed admitted" remedy only where responses are not timely served and does not condition that remedy on substance. (CCP 2023.280(a).)

The court does not find Gandall acted with substantial justification. Gandall shall pay $5,040 to Plaintiff in sanctions, payment no later than 10 court days from notice of this order.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling with a cover letter outlining compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-03-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANTS TRILOBIO INC., ROYA AMINI-NAIENI, AND MAXIMILIAN SCHOMMER's Motion To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories And For Sanctions.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The court grants the motion to compel further form interrogatory responses, denies the motion to deem objections waived, and grants sanctions.

The requests at issue are reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (CCP 2017.010.) Trilobio has shown Gandall's responses are incomplete. Gandall has not demonstrated that any objection has merit. The court orders Gandall to provide further responses to Trilobio's form interrogatories, set one, within 10 court days from notice of entry of this order.

The court denies the motion to deem Gandall's objections waived; a timely response preserves objections even where the receiving party deems them non-substantive.

The court does not find Gandall acted with substantial justification. Gandall shall pay $5,130 to Plaintiff in sanctions, payment no later than 10 court days from notice of entry of this order.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling with a cover letter outlining compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Appoint Discovery Referee
Off Calendar Feb-03-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANTS TRILOBIO INC., ROYA AMINI-NAIENI, AND MAXIMILIAN SCHOMMER's Motion To Appoint Discovery Referee.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Trilobio requests a discovery referee. Gandall opposes on the grounds that he has retained new counsel and will comply with his discovery obligations going forward. The court denies the motion without prejudice to any renewed motion for a discovery referee or further appropriate relief after Trilobio has reviewed the responses Gandall has been ordered to provide.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling with a cover letter outlining compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Documents And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-03-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANTS TRILOBIO INC., ROYA AMINI-NAIENI, AND MAXIMILIAN SCHOMMER's Motion To Compel Production Of Documents And For Sanctions.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The court grants the motion to compel further RFP responses, denies the motion to deem objections waived except as to specified RFPs, and grants sanctions.

The requests at issue are reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (CCP 2017.010.) Trilobio has shown Gandall's responses are incomplete. Gandall has not demonstrated that any objection has merit. Gandall has waived any objection that Trilobio failed to set out specific facts justifying production of documents (CCP 2031.310(b)(1).) The court orders Gandall to provide further responses to the RFPs specified in Trilobio's separate statement within 10 court days from notice of entry of this order and to produce responsive documents within 30 days of notice of entry of the order.

The court denies the motion to deem Gandall's objections waived except as to those RFPs for which no response was made by Gandall (they appear to be Nos. 35 and 36, but there is ambiguity in Trilobio's numbering of its separate statement).

The court does not find Gandall acted with substantial justification. Gandall shall pay $6,120 to Plaintiff in sanctions, payment no later than 10 court days from notice of entry of this order.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling with a cover letter outlining compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
